perturbation.original_text: Article 12 – Disposal Within ninety (90) days of termination of this Agreement, the ICC Court may request the disposal of the Confidential Information or Proprietary Information disclosed to the Member. Disposal means execution of reasonable measures to return or destroy all materials and copies including electronic data. Destruction shall be confirmed in writing. Disposa l shall be effected within thirty (30) days of the request being made.
perturbation.changed_text: Article 12 – Disposal Within ninety (90) days of termination of this Agreement, the ICC Court may request the disposal of the Confidential Information or Proprietary Information disclosed to the Member. Disposal means following ICC's protocol.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the definition of disposal means execution of reasonable measures to return or destroy all materials and copies including electronic data. Destruction shall be confirmed in writing. Disposa l shall be effected within thirty (30) days of the request being made to following ICC's protocol, this creates a contradiction on what has to be done on disposal. It creates an uncertainty as it references something that is not written in the document.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Article 12 – Disposal Within ninety (90) days of termination of this Agreement, the ICC Court may request the disposal of the Confidential Information or Proprietary Information disclosed to the Member. Disposal means following ICC's protocol.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined 'Disposal' with specific actions (return/destroy materials, confirm destruction in writing, effect disposal within 30 days). The changed text replaces this specific definition with 'following ICC's protocol,' which is undefined within the document. The contradicted text also refers to 'following ICC's protocol', so it creates uncertainty because the reader does not know what the protocol means. This omission of detail creates a direct conflict with the original intent and generates ambiguity and an in-text contradiction because what it means to follow 'ICC's protocol' is missing and it doesn't align with the original definition, hence causing a contradiction according to the definition of Omissions- In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Article 11 – Breach and Remedies The Parties agree that Confid ential Information or Proprietary Information may relate to highly sensitive aspects of ICC Court’s activities or arbitral proceedings and that loss, misuse or unauthorised Derivation or disclosure of the Confidential Information or Proprietary Information may be highly prejudicial to the interests of ICC and the ICC Court, and that financial compensation may not adequatly compensate ICC for any such damage. ICC therefore reserves the right to take whatever injunctive or other action it deems necessary, at law, in equity or otherwise to protect its interests in the event of any actual and/or alleged breach of this Agreement by the Member.
perturbation.changed_text: Article 11 – Breach and Remedies The Parties agree that Confid ential Information or Proprietary Information may relate to highly sensitive aspects of ICC Court’s activities or arbitral proceedings and that loss, misuse or unauthorised Derivation or disclosure of the Confidential Information or Proprietary Information may be highly prejudicial to the interests of ICC and the ICC Court, and that financial compensation may not adequatly compensate ICC for any such damage.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the text ICC therefore reserves the right to take whatever injunctive or other action it deems necessary, at law, in equity or otherwise to protect its interests in the event of any actual and/or alleged breach of this Agreement by the Member., while leaving the article states how damages caused to ICC may not be covered by financial compensation and the removal of the remedies, makes it not enforceable as no action can be taken.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Article 11 – Breach and Remedies The Parties agree that Confid ential Information or Proprietary Information may relate to highly sensitive aspects of ICC Court’s activities or arbitral proceedings and that loss, misuse or unauthorised Derivation or disclosure of the Confidential Information or Proprietary Information may be highly prejudicial to the interests of ICC and the ICC Court, and that financial compensation may not adequatly compensate ICC for any such damage.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Article 11 states that ICC reserves the right to take injunctive or other action in the event of a breach. The changed text removes this right, while the contradicted text (which is actually the same as the original text), states that financial compensation may not adequately compensate ICC for damages. This removal of remedies creates a contradiction because while the parties recognize financial compensation may not be adequate, and there was previously the ability to take action, that ability has been removed, creating an unenforcable and contradictory situation.

perturbation.original_text: Article 9 – Term and Termination This Agreement enters into force by signing of all Parties and can be terminated by either Party with immediate effect by giving a written notice to the other Party. This Agreement ends automatically upon expiry of the Member’s term of office.
perturbation.changed_text: Article 9 – Term and Termination This Agreement enters into force by signing of all Parties and can be terminated by ICC with immediate effect. This Agreement ends automatically upon expiry of the Member’s term of office.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the ability to terminate for the Member but keeping the ability to terminate upon ICC, gives rise to uncertainty, as it is one sided and creates in-text contradiction with Article 13 where it refers to good faith and fair dealing, not allowing to terminate the contract doesn't look like good faith and fair dealing.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Article 13 – Good Faith and Fair Dealing In carrying out their obligations under this Agreement, the Parties will act in accordance with the principles of good faith and fair dealing . The provisions of this Agreement, as well as any statements made by the Parties in connection with this Agreement, shall be interpreted in accordance with the principles of good faith and fair dealing.
perturbation.justification: The original text allows either party to terminate the agreement with written notice. The changed text removes the Member's ability to terminate, granting this right solely to the ICC. This creates a strong contradiction with Article 13, which mandates good faith and fair dealing. Restricting the Member's termination rights while granting them to the ICC raises concerns about fairness and potentially conflicts with the principle of good faith, thus the change introduces an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Article 3 – Exclusions from Obligation to keep Confidential and Restrictive Use The obligations under Article 2 to keep confidential all Confidential Information or Proprietary Information shall not apply to the extent that the Member can prove that any of that information: a) was in the Member’s possession without an obligation of confidentiality prior to receipt from the ICC Court; b) is lawfully obtained by the Member from a third party without an obligation of confidentiality, provided that third party is not, to the ICC C ourt’s best knowledge, in breach of any obligation of confidentiality to the ICC Court relating to that information; or c) is developed by the Member independent of any Confidential Information or Proprietary Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Article 3 – Exclusions from Obligation to keep Confidential and Restrictive Use The obligations under Article 2 to keep confidential all Confidential Information or Proprietary Information shall not apply.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the conditions to the exclusions of keeping confidential, the meaning of the article is lost. This creates an in-text contradiction with Article 2, which is obligating to keep confidential and restrictive use.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Article 2 – Obligation to keep Confidential and Restrictive Use The Member shall: a) not disclose any Confidential Information or Proprie tary Information unless expressly authorised by the ICC Court; b) use any Confidential Information or Proprietary Information exclusively for the purpose of its collaboration with the ICC Court and not for its own or anyone else’s benefit; c) keep secu re, confidential and hold all Confidential Information and Proprietary Information with no less a degree of care as is used for the Member’s own confidential or Proprietary Information and at least with reasonable care; and d) not amend, vary, develop, im prove, enhance (“Derivation”) the Confidential Information or Proprietary Information except as otherwise approved in writing by the ICC Court. In addition, ICC Court members are only permitted to disclose Confidential information or Proprietary informati on for the sole purpose of conflict checks. They must only disclose such information to the person duly authorized to run conflict checks within their respective law firm or company. ICC Court members undertake that their firm or company will not use the C onfidential information or Proprietary information for any other purpose and remain responsible for ensuring that such information is immediately destroyed upon conclusion of the conflict check.
perturbation.justification: The original text of Article 3 provided specific conditions under which the obligation to keep information confidential would not apply. The changed text removes all those conditions, stating that Article 2's confidentiality obligations simply do not apply. This directly contradicts Article 2, which outlines the Member's obligation to keep Confidential Information confidential and restrictive use, as now Article 3 states the obligation to keep confidential and restrictive use is not in effect. This constitutes a strong in-text contradiction based on the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' definition.
